title: Healthcare M&A winners, losers
full_text:
The federal budget has created clear winners and losers in the healthcare mergers and acquisitions landscape.
Pathology group Healius has issued a profit downgrade that may derail takeover interest, while private equity-owned ForHealth stands to benefit from $1.8bn in urgent care clinic funds.
Healius on Tuesday downgraded its full-year underlying earnings before interest and tax guidance to $30m-$35m, significantly below the $44m consensus forecast and down as much as 38 per cent from prior guidance. Its shares tumbled more than 19 per cent, taking the market value of the once $2bn company to just over $350m.
The news comes as a private equity suitor had been waiting for the budget to determine pathology funding arrangements before making a move, with sources previously indicating interest if Healius shares stayed below 70c.
But the budget delivered no new funding for pathology, and the government is actually planning to save $146.3m over two years through reduced spending on duplicative diagnostic imaging and pathology tests.
The funding squeeze, combined with significant wage increases from Fair Work Commission decisions on gender-based undervaluation, leaves Healius in a precarious position. The company said inadequate funding could force the sector to start charging out-of-pocket fees for pathology tests.
In contrast, BGH Capital’s ForHealth primary healthcare business appears well-positioned after the budget locked in $1.8bn over five years for Medicare Urgent Care Clinics on an ongoing basis.
BGH purchased the business from Healius for about $500m in 2020, when it was called Primary Health Care. Meanwhile, private-hospital operators such as Healthscope and Ramsay Health Care have been dealt a blow with the removal of the private health insurance rebate for people aged 65 and over from April 2027.
summary: The federal budget has negatively impacted Healius with a profit downgrade and no new funding for pathology, while ForHealth benefits from $1.8bn in funding for urgent care clinics.
author: BRIDGET CARTER, DATA ROOM
heading: Health and Disability Insurance
key_points: Healius downgraded its earnings guidance to $30m-$35m, causing shares to drop over 19%.
The budget includes $1.8bn for Medicare Urgent Care Clinics, benefiting ForHealth.
Private hospital operators face challenges due to the removal of the private health insurance rebate for seniors from April 2027.
